nodes.label: P5.1 — objective sharpness: temperature is the free, direction-driven depth lever
nodes.rationale: Committed temp=0.2 (tail 0.530, readout 0.550 beats tuned-BP 0.531); the lr-matched control proves ~82% of the lift is direction not a disguised learning-rate; temp0.05 collapses from intrinsic over-sharpening, not negative-starvation; adoption pended on the P5.7 continual gate.

nodes.label: P5.1 verdict: temperature 0.2 is a free, direction-driven depth lever (committed)
nodes.rationale: Sharper InfoNCE temperature (0.5->0.2) lifts tail-L12 0.435->0.530 and readout 0.449->0.550 (beating tuned-BP 0.531), marching the peak L5->L6; committed pending the P5.7 continual-safety gate.

nodes.label: The lr-matched control: ~82% of the temperature lift is direction, not learning-rate
nodes.rationale: A temp=0.5 arm re-tuned to temp0.2's effective gradient-norm (step-norm ratio 2.249, lr=0.0675) isolates the learning-rate confound; temp0.2 (0.530) beats the lr-matched arm (0.452) 5/5 IQR-disjoint, so the majority of the +0.095 lift is hard-negative reweighting (direction), not a bigger step — validates the spine mechanistically.

nodes.label: The temperature floor: temp0.05 collapses from intrinsic over-sharpening, not negative-starvation
nodes.rationale: A batch-size control (batch64 vs batch32 at temp0.05) does not rescue the collapse (0.491 vs 0.485, no effect) — the safe-sharpness floor is an objective property (over-sharpening concentrates on the single hardest negative regardless of count), bounding how far temperature can be pushed.
